payload.source_text: The pocket money tracker system allows children to claim pocket money tasks as they are completed throughout the week.
Parents can detail tasks that should be completed and the monetary amount earned for that task.
As a task is completed it is linked to the child that completed it.
The children should be able to see the available tasks and the tasks they have completed that week.
At the end of each week the children can see the total amount they have earned that week.
The Parents can also see a report of how much each child has earned and the tasks they completed so that they can provide the pocket money earned to each child.
At the start of the next week all tasks become available again to be claimed.
Historical task data shall be available.
The resulting system shall be able to operate on iOS 16, Android 9 and Windows 11 or later.

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL Source elements present in the ledger.

payload.evaluated_scope: All input material in this project

original_descriptions: Task completed by child for reward eligibility
Pocket money earned through task completion
Enable children to claim earned pocket money for completed tasks
Establish task-based monetary reward structure for children
Provide children visibility into earning opportunities and achievements
Enable children to understand weekly earning outcomes
Provide parents control over task definition and monetary allocation
Enable parent oversight of child earnings for payment decisions

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL ZachmanCell elements present in the ledger.

payload.description: Pocket money earned through task completion for reward eligibility
payload.justification: Signals reference tasks as strategic entities central to the reward mechanism, representing work units completed for pocket money. | Signals identify pocket money as strategic entity representing monetary value claimed and earned through task completion outcomes.

payload.description: Weekly earning and accountability cycle
payload.justification: Signals reference weekly timeframe as strategic cycle governing task completion periods and earning outcome assessment.

payload.description: Child stakeholder claiming and earning pocket money
payload.justification: Multiple signals reference children as primary actors claiming money, viewing opportunities, and understanding earnings in the strategic context.

payload.description: Parent stakeholder establishing rewards and providing oversight
payload.justification: Signals identify parents as strategic actors responsible for establishing reward structures and maintaining accountability oversight.

payload.description: Enable children to claim earned pocket money through a task-based monetary reward structure
payload.justification: Signal explicitly expresses core strategic purpose of enabling task-based pocket money claiming mechanism for children. | Signal defines parental strategic motivation to create structured reward system linking tasks to monetary compensation.

payload.description: Provide children visibility into earning opportunities, achievements, and weekly earning outcomes
payload.justification: Signal articulates motivational objective focused on transparency of earning potential and accomplishment tracking for children. | Signal establishes strategic purpose of providing children comprehension of their accumulated weekly earnings.

payload.description: Enable parental oversight for accountability and reward commitment fulfillment
payload.justification: Signal defines strategic objective for parental monitoring capability to ensure accountability and adherence to reward promises.

payload.justification: Business process whereby parents define tasks and assign monetary values.

payload.description: Weekly earnings aggregation for children
payload.justification: Business process of aggregating monetary totals on weekly basis.

payload.description: Parent reviewing child earnings and completed tasks for payment processing
payload.justification: Business process for parent reporting and payment processing described in signal.

payload.description: Task visibility function for child users
payload.justification: Business function providing visibility into available and completed tasks.

payload.description: Earnings reporting function for parents
payload.justification: Business function enabling parent visibility into child earnings and task completion.

payload.description: Task availability reset occurs on weekly cycle boundaries
payload.justification: Temporal business rule governing task availability state transitions.

payload.description: Historical task records must be retained beyond current period
payload.justification: Data retention business rule specified in signal.

payload.description: Task business entity with defined monetary value
payload.justification: Signal SG922 explicitly describes business entity capability for tasks with monetary values - clear Entity at business context level.

payload.description: Child business entity earning pocket money
payload.justification: Multiple signals reference child as a distinct business entity within the pocket money system context.

payload.description: Parent business entity defining and monitoring tasks
payload.justification: Signals describe parent as distinct business entity with task definition and reporting capabilities.

payload.description: Task completion status attribute
payload.justification: Signals distinguish between completed and available tasks, indicating completion status as key business attribute.

payload.description: Task monetary value attribute
payload.justification: Signals explicitly reference monetary values associated with tasks and earnings aggregation.

payload.description: Task availability state attribute
payload.justification: Signals reference available tasks and weekly reset of availability state.

payload.description: Weekly earned monetary total attribute for child
payload.justification: Signal SG925 describes aggregated weekly earnings as business-level attribute.

payload.description: Child claims completed task relationship
payload.justification: Core business relationship between child and completed tasks explicitly stated in signals.

payload.description: Parent defines task relationship
payload.justification: Signal establishes parent capability to define tasks, indicating ownership relationship.

payload.description: Task historical record retention relationship
payload.justification: Signal indicates temporal relationship between current and historical task records.

payload.description: Task completion event
payload.justification: Task completion represents significant business event enabling claims and earnings.

payload.description: Weekly business cycle for task availability and earnings aggregation
payload.justification: Signals explicitly describe weekly cycle governing task reset and earnings totals.

payload.description: Weekly cycle boundary triggering task availability reset
payload.justification: Weekly boundary acts as temporal trigger for state reset as described in signal.

payload.description: Child actor claiming tasks and earning pocket money
payload.justification: Child explicitly identified as primary actor interacting with tasks and earnings.

payload.description: Parent actor defining tasks and processing payments
payload.justification: Parent explicitly identified as actor with task definition and payment processing responsibilities.

payload.description: Enable children to earn pocket money through task completion
payload.justification: Core business purpose and goal stated in signal describing pocket money allocation system.

payload.description: Provide parents control over task definition, monetary allocation, and oversight of child earnings for payment decisions
payload.justification: Business goal of enabling parental control over task parameters and values. | Business goal of providing reporting capability for payment processing decisions.

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL CellContentItem elements present in the ledger.

payload.name: Family Participation
payload.description: Enterprise concern defining who participates in the household economy system and the nature of their relationships—children as claimants and earners, parents as overseers and reward authorities.

payload.name: Earned Entitlement Governance
payload.description: Enterprise commitment to a task-based monetary reward structure through which children gain entitlement to pocket money, including the accountability cycle and earning opportunities that govern this exchange.

payload.name: Transparency Obligation
payload.description: Enterprise responsibility to provide visibility into earning opportunities, achievement recognition, and outcome disclosure, ensuring both earners and overseers have access to performance and reward information.

payload.name: Task Stewardship
payload.description: Business responsibility for establishing, maintaining, and governing the task catalog including definition of work obligations, associated value assignments, and availability lifecycle across business cycles.

payload.name: Participation Entitlement
payload.description: Business responsibility for enabling and governing child participation in earning opportunities through task claim rights, completion accountability, and entitlement to monetary recognition for fulfilled obligations.

payload.name: Earnings Settlement
payload.description: Business responsibility for periodic aggregation and reconciliation of completed work value into settlement-ready earnings totals, including oversight obligations and payment decision authority.

payload.name: Historical Record Stewardship
payload.description: Business responsibility for preservation and retention of task completion records beyond active business periods to satisfy accountability, auditability, and governance obligations.

payload.description: Expresses the core purpose enabling children to claim pocket money for completed tasks throughout the week.
Defines the parental motivation to establish task-based monetary reward structures for children.
Articulates the motivational objective of providing children visibility into earning opportunities and achievements.
Establishes the purpose of enabling children to understand their weekly earning outcomes.
Defines parental oversight objective for accountability and fulfillment of reward commitments.
Header indicating the start of a conceptual-level system description.
Core business purpose describing children claiming completed tasks for pocket money allocation.
Business entity capability enabling parents to define tasks with associated monetary values.
Conceptual relationship establishing linkage between completed tasks and child entities.
Child actor visibility requirement covering both available and completed task entities.
Weekly aggregation capability showing earned monetary totals for child entities.
Parent reporting capability providing visibility into child earnings and completed tasks for payment processing.
Temporal business rule resetting task availability state on weekly cycle boundaries.
Data retention requirement for temporal task entity records beyond current period.
